pages.text: An endgame lane that copies items the frogs never had a slime for - odd vanilla bits and modded resources alike. Feed one in, get many back. It only takes items with no slime of their own, so it never shortcuts the normal chains; it just fills the gaps.

The $(l)Alembic$() takes an empty bucket and any off-roster item (plus power) and gives back a bucket holding a $(o)Mimic Slime$() that copies it. Plain items only - anything with special data (a named tool, a written book, a potion) is refused, shown by a red X over the arrow.

Milk a Mimic Slime Bucket in a $(l)Slime Milker$() to get $(o)Mimic Milk$(). Pour it out and it spawns a crowd of Mimic Slimes - the same one-into-many trick the normal milk pools do. That is where the copies come from.

The slimes are eaten by $(l)Midas$(), a frog of legend. Kiss unhatched frogspawn with a $(o)Princess's Kiss$() to wake one (the Kiss drops from the dragon). Midas only eats Mimic Slimes, breeds true, and the more $(o)Bounty$() you breed into it, the more it drops per slime.
Midas drops a $(o)Prismatic Froglight$() for each slime it eats. Put it in the $(l)Distiller$() with power and it renders back the original item. One leftover item in, a steady stream of it out.
